label: Google Docs
description: Arcade.dev LLM tools for Google Docs

tools.name: CommentOnDocument
tools.description: Comment on a specific document by its ID.

parameters.name: document_id
parameters.description: The ID of the document to comment on

parameters.name: comment_text
parameters.description: The comment to add to the document

output.description: The comment's ID, documentId, and documentUrl in a dictionary

comment_text.value: This section needs more clarity. Please revise the third paragraph to better explain the methodology.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: CreateBlankDocument
tools.description: Create a blank Google Docs document with the specified title.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The title of the blank document to create

output.description: The created document's title, documentId, and documentUrl in a dictionary

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: CreateDocumentFromText
tools.description:
Create a Google Docs document with the specified title and text content.

When input_format is MARKDOWN, the text_content is parsed as Markdown and the resulting
document is formatted with headings, bold, italic, bullet lists, and numbered lists.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The title of the document to create

parameters.name: text_content
parameters.description: The text content to insert into the document. When input_format is MARKDOWN, supports Markdown formatting: headings (# through ######), bold (**text**), italic (*text*), bullet lists (- item), and numbered lists (1. item).

parameters.name: input_format
parameters.description: The format of text_content. MARKDOWN applies rich formatting (headings, bold, italic, bullet lists, numbered lists). PLAIN inserts text as-is with no formatting. Defaults to MARKDOWN.

output.description: The created document's title, documentId, and documentUrl in a dictionary

text_content.value:
# Project Kickoff Meeting

**Date:** October 15, 2024

## Agenda

- Introduction and team overview
- Project goals and timeline
- Task assignments

## Key Decisions

1. Launch date set for Q1 2025
2. Weekly syncs every Monday at 10am
3. Use Slack for daily communication

## Next Steps

*All team members* should review the project brief before the next meeting.

### Action Items

- Alice: Finalize requirements document
- Bob: Set up the development environment
- Carol: Schedule stakeholder interviews

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: EditDocument
tools.description:
Read or edit a Google Docs document using structured batchUpdate requests.

When called without requests, returns the document content in DocMD format (block IDs,
character indices, and text styles). When called with requests, applies the edits and
returns the updated DocMD. Use the DocMD indices from the response to construct
requests for subsequent calls.

parameters.name: document_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Google Docs document to edit.

parameters.name: requests

output.description: The result including document ID, URL, number of applied edits, and the document content in DocMD format.

insertText.text: This section describes the key findings.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GenerateGoogleFilePickerUrl
tools.description:
Generate a URL where the user can grant this app access to specific Drive files.

Opens Google's first-party Drive picker. The user selects which files to share
with this application — it is not a sign-in or credential prompt.

Use this when a prior tool reported that a file was not found or access was denied,
and the user expects the file to exist. After the user completes the picker flow,
retry the prior tool.

output.description: Google File Picker URL for user file selection and permission granting

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GetDocumentAsDocmd
tools.description:
Get the latest version of the specified Google Docs document as DocMD.
The DocMD output will include tags that can be used to annotate the document with location
information, the type of block, block IDs, and other metadata. If the document has tabs,
all tabs are included in sequential order unless a specific tab_id is provided.

parameters.name: document_id
parameters.description: The ID of the document to retrieve.

parameters.name: tab_id
parameters.description: The ID of a specific tab to retrieve. If provided, returns only content from that tab. If omitted, returns all tabs in sequential depth-first order.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GetDocumentById
tools.description:
DEPRECATED DO NOT USE THIS TOOL
Get the latest version of the specified Google Docs document.

parameters.name: document_id
parameters.description: The ID of the document to retrieve.

output.description: The document contents as a dictionary

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GetDocumentMetadata
tools.description:
Get metadata for a Google Docs document including hierarchical tab structure.
Returns document title, ID, URL, total character count, and nested tab information
with character counts for each tab.

parameters.name: document_id
parameters.description: The ID of the document to get metadata for

output.description: Document metadata including hierarchical tab structure

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: InsertTextAtEndOfDocument
tools.description: Updates an existing Google Docs document using the batchUpdate API endpoint.

parameters.name: document_id
parameters.description: The ID of the document to update.

parameters.name: text_content
parameters.description: The text content to insert into the document

output.description: The response from the batchUpdate API as a dict.

text_content.value: This is an example paragraph appended to the end of the document.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: ListDocumentComments
tools.description: List all comments on the specified Google Docs document.

parameters.name: document_id
parameters.description: The ID of the document to list comments for

parameters.name: include_deleted
parameters.description: Whether to include deleted comments in the results. Defaults to False.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: SearchAndRetrieveDocuments
tools.description:
Searches for documents in the user's Google Drive and returns documents with their main body
content and tab metadata. Excludes documents that are in the trash.

Returns main body content only with metadata about tabs. Use get_document_as_docmd() to retrieve
full tab content for specific documents. Use search_documents() for metadata-only searches.

parameters.name: return_format
parameters.description: The format of the document to return. Defaults to Markdown.

parameters.name: document_contains
parameters.description: Keywords or phrases that must be in the document title or body. Provide a list of keywords or phrases if needed.

parameters.name: document_not_contains
parameters.description: Keywords or phrases that must NOT be in the document title or body. Provide a list of keywords or phrases if needed.

parameters.name: search_only_in_shared_drive_id
parameters.description: The ID of the shared drive to restrict the search to. If provided, the search will only return documents from this drive. Defaults to None, which searches across all drives.

parameters.name: include_shared_drives
parameters.description: Whether to include documents from shared drives. Defaults to False (searches only in the user's 'My Drive').

parameters.name: include_organization_domain_documents
parameters.description: Whether to include documents from the organization's domain. This is applicable to admin users who have permissions to view organization-wide documents in a Google Workspace account. Defaults to False.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Sort order. Defaults to listing the most recently modified documents first

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of documents to list

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token to continue a previous request

output.description: A dictionary containing document count, list of documents with content and metadata, pagination token, and has_more flag

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: SearchDocuments
tools.description:
Searches for documents in the user's Google Drive. Excludes documents in trash.
Returns metadata only. Use get_document_metadata or get_document_as_docmd for content.

parameters.name: document_contains
parameters.description: Keywords or phrases that must be in the document title or body. Provide a list of keywords or phrases if needed.

parameters.name: document_not_contains
parameters.description: Keywords or phrases that must NOT be in the document title or body. Provide a list of keywords or phrases if needed.

parameters.name: search_only_in_shared_drive_id
parameters.description: The ID of the shared drive to restrict the search to. If provided, the search will only return documents from this drive. Defaults to None, which searches across all drives.

parameters.name: include_shared_drives
parameters.description: Whether to include documents from shared drives. Defaults to False (searches only in the user's 'My Drive').

parameters.name: include_organization_domain_documents
parameters.description: Whether to include documents from the organization's domain. This is applicable to admin users who have permissions to view organization-wide documents in a Google Workspace account. Defaults to False.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Sort order. Defaults to listing the most recently modified documents first. If document_contains or document_not_contains is provided, then the order_by will be ignored.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of documents to list

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token to continue a previous request

output.description: Document count, list of documents, pagination token, and has_more flag

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: WhoAmI
tools.description:
Get comprehensive user profile and Google Docs environment information.

This tool provides detailed information about the authenticated user including
their name, email, profile picture, Google Docs access permissions, and other
important profile details from Google services.

output.description: Get comprehensive user profile and Google Docs environment information.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

documentationChunks.content:
<Callout type="warning">
  This Toolkit is not available in Arcade Cloud. You can use these tools with a
  [self-hosted](/guides/deployment-hosting/configure-engine) instance of Arcade.
</Callout>

## Tab Support

Google Docs supports hierarchical tabs within documents. The Google Docs tools provide comprehensive support for working with tabs:

- **Tab Metadata**: `GetDocumentMetadata` returns hierarchical tab structures with approximate character and word counts for each tab
- **Tab Content**: `GetDocumentAsDocMD` and `SearchAndRetrieveDocuments` include all tab content in their output
- **Tab Filtering**: `GetDocumentAsDocMD` supports filtering to retrieve content from a specific tab using the `tab_id` parameter

Tabs are represented with the following structure:
- Each tab has a unique `tabId`, `title`, `index`, and `nestingLevel`
- Tabs can be nested up to 3 levels deep (parent → child → grandchild)
- Tab metadata includes approximate character and word counts for each tab's content

---

The Arcade Google Docs MCP Server uses the [Google auth provider](/references/auth-providers/google) to connect to users' Google accounts.
---

summary:
## Google Docs Toolkit

The Google Docs toolkit lets LLM agents create, read, edit, search, and annotate Google Docs documents through the Google Docs and Drive APIs via Arcade.

## Capabilities

- **Document creation:** Create blank documents or populate them from plain text or Markdown (with automatic heading, bold, italic, and list formatting).
- **Document reading & metadata:** Retrieve full document content in DocMD format (block IDs, character indices, text styles, tab structure) or fetch metadata-only (title, ID, URL, character counts, tab hierarchy).
- **Structured editing:** Apply batchUpdate requests using DocMD indices for precise in-document edits; append text to document ends.
- **Search & discovery:** Search Drive for documents by query, with options to return metadata only or include full body content; trash is automatically excluded.
- **Comments:** Add new comments to a document or list all existing comments.
- **User & access management:** Retrieve authenticated user profile and permissions; generate a Google Drive inline file picker URL to grant per-file access when a document is not found or access is denied.

## OAuth

This toolkit uses OAuth 2.0 with **Google** as the provider. See the [Arcade Google auth provider docs](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/references/auth-providers/google) for setup details.

## Secrets

- **`ENABLE_GOOGLE_DRIVE_INLINE_PICKER_URL`** — A flag/URL secret that activates the `GenerateGoogleFilePickerUrl` tool, which opens Google's first-party Drive file picker so users can grant the app access to specific files without a full re-authentication flow. To obtain or configure this value, you set it in your Arcade environment to enable the picker endpoint. Refer to the [Arcade secrets documentation](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/guides/create-tools/tool-basics/create-tool-secrets) for how to define secrets, and manage them at [https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets](https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets).
